proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (a continuous variable spanning 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values of feature_078 correspond to longer progression-free survival.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=0.785, p=0.00e+00; Pearson r=0.766, p=0.00e+00. Quartile means of pfs_months by feature_078 quartile: Q1=2.09, Q2=3.71, Q3=4.90, Q4=6.59. A clear monotonic gradient confirms a strong positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary marker present in ~55% of patients) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean PFS than those with feature_051=0.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: mean PFS = 3.703 (n=27481) for feature_051=1 vs 5.055 (n=22519) for feature_051=0; mean difference = -1.352 months, p=0.00e+00. OLS beta=-1.352, p=0.00e+00. feature_051 is by far the strongest individual predictor of shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 (binary marker present in ~20% of patients) is associated with longer pfs_months: patients with feature_038=1 have higher mean PFS than those with feature_038=0; this could represent a beneficial therapy or favorable biomarker.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: mean PFS = 5.089 (n=10022) for feature_038=1 vs 4.117 (n=39978) for feature_038=0; mean difference = +0.972 months, p=1.71e-216. feature_038 is the strongest single feature associated with LONGER PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2) shows a monotonic decreasing dose-response with pfs_months: higher levels of feature_057 correspond to shorter PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Means by level: 0=5.271, 1=4.054, 2=2.908. ANOVA F=3528.9, p=0.00e+00. Spearman rho=-0.360, p=0.00e+00. Strong monotonic decrease consistent with dose-response (e.g., disease stage or grade).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the other 5-level ordinal features (feature_025, feature_075, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033 — possible severity / stage / line-of-therapy ladders), at least one shows a monotonic association with pfs_months.
pfs_months differs across race groups recorded in feature_064 (white / hispanic / black / asian / other), suggesting potential demographic disparities in disease course or treatment response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by race: asian=4.306(n=3003), black=4.273(n=5996), hispanic=4.315(n=7572), other=4.407(n=973), white=4.316(n=32456). ANOVA F=0.87, p=4.79e-01. Pairwise vs white: hispanic: diff=-0.001 p=9.72e-01, black: diff=-0.042 p=1.89e-01, asian: diff=-0.010 p=8.21e-01, other: diff=+0.091 p=2.48e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months differs across insurance categories recorded in feature_018 (medicare / private / medicaid / uninsured), with non-private insurance associated with shorter PFS (a healthcare-disparity pattern).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by insurance: medicaid=4.277(n=6991), medicare=4.312(n=21105), private=4.330(n=19898), uninsured=4.249(n=2006). ANOVA F=1.47, p=2.19e-01. Pairwise vs private: medicare: diff=-0.019 p=4.09e-01, medicaid: diff=-0.053 p=9.80e-02, uninsured: diff=-0.082 p=1.25e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on the top univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109, feature_067 plus race/insurance), each of feature_078, feature_051 and feature_038 retains an independent association with pfs_months (collinearity does not erase them).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.8584. Independent coefficients: feature_078: beta=+0.1758 p=0.00e+00; feature_057: beta=-1.1715 p=0.00e+00; feature_051: beta=-1.3614 p=0.00e+00; feature_038: beta=+0.9428 p=0.00e+00; feature_099: beta=-0.0761 p=0.00e+00; feature_092: beta=+0.4694 p=0.00e+00; feature_013: beta=-0.3533 p=0.00e+00; feature_043: beta=-0.2869 p=7.82e-273; feature_009: beta=-0.0050 p=2.66e-97; feature_109: beta=-0.3556 p=1.18e-78; feature_018_uninsured: beta=-0.0269 p=2.18e-01; feature_067: beta=+0.0209 p=3.61e-01; feature_064_white: beta=-0.0109 p=5.06e-01; feature_018_medicare: beta=-0.0074 p=5.35e-01; feature_064_other: beta=+0.0177 p=5.79e-01. 10 predictors significant at p<0.001.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_038 and feature_051 on pfs_months: the magnitude of the feature_038 benefit differs between patients with feature_051=1 vs feature_051=0 (potential treatment-by-biomarker effect modification).

analyses.result_summary:
Stratum means (n in cell):
  feature_051=0: feature_038=0 mean=4.868 (n=18049); feature_038=1 mean=5.809 (n=4470); diff=+0.941
  feature_051=1: feature_038=0 mean=3.499 (n=21929); feature_038=1 mean=4.509 (n=5552); diff=+1.011
Interaction term feature_038:feature_051 beta=+0.070, p=1.51e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs across race groups (feature_064): the effect of feature_038=1 vs 0 is larger or smaller in some racial subgroups, suggesting differential response or differential access/quality of treatment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_064)*feature_038', data=df).fit(); ANOVA vs additive model

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs across insurance categories (feature_018): patients with private insurance experience a different magnitude of feature_038 benefit than those with medicare, medicaid, or uninsured status.

analyses.result_summary: Effect of feature_038 within insurance (mean diff, p, n1, n0): medicare: +0.965, p=6.51e-92, n1=4265, n0=16840; private: +0.935, p=3.67e-83, n1=3992, n0=15906; uninsured: +0.933, p=7.09e-09, n1=382, n0=1624; medicaid: +1.112, p=5.36e-38, n1=1383, n0=5608. Joint interaction (insurance x feature_038) F=1.74, p=1.56e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs by level of feature_057 (the 3-level severity-like ordinal): the absolute treatment effect (mean PFS difference) is larger in patients with higher feature_057 levels (more severe disease).

The PFS benefit of feature_038 is modified by the continuous biomarker feature_092 (range 1.5-5.5): there is a significant feature_038 x feature_092 interaction in OLS, with larger benefit at higher feature_092.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta (feature_038 x feature_092) = -0.0770, p=1.25e-01. Stratified by feature_092 median split: feature_038 effect in low-feature_092 = +1.033; in high-feature_092 = +0.926.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among rare (~3-5% prevalence) binary markers (feature_067, feature_109, feature_035, feature_036, feature_027), at least one is associated with a clinically meaningful difference in pfs_months despite low prevalence.

analyses.result_summary: feature_067 (n1=1506, prevalence=0.030): mean PFS = 4.526 (carriers) vs 4.305 (non), diff=+0.221 months, p=7.31e-04.
feature_109 (n1=2272, prevalence=0.045): mean PFS = 4.094 (carriers) vs 4.322 (non), diff=-0.228 months, p=3.47e-07.
feature_035 (n1=2513, prevalence=0.050): mean PFS = 4.293 (carriers) vs 4.313 (non), diff=-0.019 months, p=6.80e-01.
feature_036 (n1=1504, prevalence=0.030): mean PFS = 4.258 (carriers) vs 4.313 (non), diff=-0.056 months, p=3.60e-01.
feature_027 (n1=251, prevalence=0.005): mean PFS = 4.351 (carriers) vs 4.311 (non), diff=+0.040 months, p=7.86e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (a heavily right-skewed continuous variable, range 0.02-777) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values correspond to shorter PFS, consistent with a tumor-burden / inflammation marker.

Among the broader panel of continuous lab/vital-like features not yet tested (feature_006, feature_070, feature_044, feature_084, feature_028, feature_065, feature_118, feature_056, feature_103, feature_094, feature_121, feature_019, feature_059, feature_003, feature_010, feature_101, feature_020, feature_090, feature_119, feature_054, feature_062, feature_031, feature_082, feature_097, feature_016, feature_042, feature_123, feature_055, feature_011, feature_014), few have any independent monotonic association with pfs_months (most are noise relative to PFS).

After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109, feature_067), residual race-group differences in pfs_months persist (i.e., race is not fully mediated by the measured clinical features).
After the same adjustment, residual insurance-group differences in pfs_months persist.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (vs reference, with p-values): feature_064_black: beta=-0.0020, p=9.18e-01; feature_064_hispanic: beta=-0.0035, p=8.52e-01; feature_064_other: beta=+0.0177, p=5.79e-01; feature_064_white: beta=-0.0109, p=5.06e-01

Adjusted insurance coefficients (vs reference, with p-values): feature_018_medicare: beta=-0.0074, p=5.35e-01; feature_018_private: beta=-0.0036, p=7.63e-01; feature_018_uninsured: beta=-0.0269, p=2.18e-01

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 varies across quartiles of feature_078: the absolute mean PFS difference (feature_038=1 minus feature_038=0) differs by feature_078 quartile, indicating effect modification by feature_078.
The negative impact of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across race groups (feature_064): the magnitude of the feature_051 detrimental effect is heterogeneous by race.

The prevalence of feature_038 (the strongest beneficial marker, possibly representing a therapy or favorable treatment) differs across race groups (feature_064), suggesting unequal access or assignment by demographic group.
The prevalence of feature_038 differs across insurance categories (feature_018), suggesting unequal access by insurance.

analyses.result_summary: Prevalence of feature_038 by race: asian=0.211, black=0.201, hispanic=0.198, other=0.192, white=0.200. Chi^2=3.05, p=5.50e-01.
Prevalence of feature_038 by insurance: medicaid=0.198, medicare=0.202, private=0.201, uninsured=0.190. Chi^2=1.91, p=5.91e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way interaction between feature_038, feature_051, and race (feature_064) on pfs_months: the magnitude of the feature_038 benefit within feature_051=1 patients differs across race groups, suggesting compounded effect modification by demographic group.

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=white, feature_051=0: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=white, feature_051=0: feature_038 effect = +0.915 months (p=4.98e-59); n1=2894, n0=11820

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=white, feature_051=1: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=white, feature_051=1: feature_038 effect = +0.950 months (p=1.23e-83); n1=3599, n0=14143

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=hispanic, feature_051=0: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=hispanic, feature_051=0: feature_038 effect = +1.153 months (p=2.72e-21); n1=668, n0=2680

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=hispanic, feature_051=1: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=hispanic, feature_051=1: feature_038 effect = +1.073 months (p=6.22e-24); n1=831, n0=3393

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=asian, feature_051=0: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=asian, feature_051=0: feature_038 effect = +0.898 months (p=7.27e-07); n1=285, n0=1056

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=asian, feature_051=1: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=asian, feature_051=1: feature_038 effect = +1.239 months (p=5.91e-14); n1=350, n0=1312

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=black, feature_051=0: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=black, feature_051=0: feature_038 effect = +0.835 months (p=4.22e-11); n1=537, n0=2134

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=black, feature_051=1: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=black, feature_051=1: feature_038 effect = +1.082 months (p=9.62e-20); n1=671, n0=2654

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=other, feature_051=0: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=other, feature_051=0: feature_038 effect = +1.005 months (p=2.98e-03); n1=86, n0=359

analyses.code: 3-way subgroup: race=other, feature_051=1: effect of feature_038
analyses.result_summary: race=other, feature_051=1: feature_038 effect = +1.398 months (p=6.50e-06); n1=101, n0=427

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*feature_051*C(feature_064)', data=df); ANOVA test of 3-way
analyses.result_summary: Joint 3-way interaction (feature_038 x feature_051 x race) F=1.54, p=1.87e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among demographic-balance binary features (feature_007, feature_066, feature_002, feature_047, feature_032, feature_063), at least one (likely a sex-like or laterality marker) is associated with pfs_months.

Race-group differences in pfs_months are partially mediated by differential prevalence of feature_038: race coefficients shrink (move toward zero) when feature_038 is added to a regression alongside race, insurance, feature_078, feature_051, and feature_057.
Insurance-group differences in pfs_months are partially mediated by differential prevalence of feature_038: insurance coefficients shrink when feature_038 is added to the same regression.

analyses.code: compare race coefficients in models with vs without feature_038
analyses.result_summary: Race coefficients (without feature_038 -> with feature_038, attenuation): C(feature_064)[T.black]: -0.0042 -> +0.0051 (delta=-0.0093); C(feature_064)[T.hispanic]: -0.0214 -> -0.0087 (delta=-0.0127); C(feature_064)[T.other]: +0.0133 -> +0.0314 (delta=-0.0181); C(feature_064)[T.white]: -0.0168 -> -0.0062 (delta=-0.0106)

analyses.code: compare insurance coefficients in models with vs without feature_038
analyses.result_summary: Insurance coefficients (without feature_038 -> with feature_038, attenuation): C(feature_018)[T.medicare]: +0.0021 -> -0.0019 (delta=+0.0040); C(feature_018)[T.private]: +0.0056 -> +0.0031 (delta=+0.0025); C(feature_018)[T.uninsured]: -0.0458 -> -0.0389 (delta=-0.0070)

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_051 x feature_038 interaction, feature_092 x feature_038 interaction, and core covariates (feature_099, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109, feature_067, race, insurance) explains substantially more variance in pfs_months than the additive feature_078-only baseline, and the feature_051:feature_038 interaction term is non-zero (effect of feature_038 differs by feature_051 status even after multivariable adjustment).

analyses.code: pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051*feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092*feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS R^2=0.8584. Key term estimates (beta, p): feature_038: +1.2232, p=2.28e-60; feature_051: -1.3686, p=0.00e+00; feature_051:feature_038: +0.0357, p=6.58e-02; feature_092: +0.4852, p=0.00e+00; feature_092:feature_038: -0.0791, p=4.12e-05; feature_078: +0.1758, p=0.00e+00; feature_057: -1.1715, p=0.00e+00. Interaction feature_051:feature_038 beta=+0.0357, p=6.58e-02. Interaction feature_092:feature_038 beta=-0.0791, p=4.12e-05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The main-effect estimates (feature_038 benefit, feature_051 harm, feature_078 positive correlation with pfs_months) replicate in two random halves of the dataset (split-half validation), indicating they are not artifacts of overfitting or sampling chance.

The residuals of the comprehensive multivariable OLS model are approximately normally distributed (Shapiro-Wilk on a 5000-row sample), supporting the validity of the OLS inference framework used in earlier iterations.

analyses.code: random split into halves A and B; recompute three main effects in each
analyses.result_summary: Half A: feature_038 effect=+0.953, feature_051 effect=-1.333, feature_078 rho=+0.787. Half B: feature_038 effect=+0.991, feature_051 effect=-1.371, feature_078 rho=+0.783. Effects replicate with high concordance.

Shapiro-Wilk on 5000-row sample of residuals: p=3.45e-72. (With n=5000 even tiny departures are flagged; visual assessment of QQ would be the right adjudicator, but the R^2 results are reasonable as descriptive summaries regardless.)
